actions.replacementText: This Agreement governs all Subscription Services and all executed Order Forms. Additional procurement terms are rejected unless expressly accepted in writing by Provider.

actions.supplementDraft: In the event of conflict between this Agreement and any Order Form, the Order Form prevails only for commercial variables expressly identified in that Order Form.

actions.replacementText: Order Forms prevail over procurement documents. Any purchase-order terms conflicting with this Agreement are void unless Provider expressly accepts them in writing.

actions.supplementDraft: Provider shall include in each invoice a line-item summary of recurring subscription, variable usage, and one-off professional service charges.
SLA metrics and service-credit calculations shall be measured using Provider monitoring systems, subject to Customer's right to request supporting evidence.

actions.replacementText: Provider will provide monthly SLA performance reports and identify excluded downtime categories used in uptime calculations.
Service credits are Customer's sole monetary remedy for isolated SLA shortfalls, but repeated failures across three consecutive months constitute material breach under Section 4.4.

actions.supplementDraft: For Severity 1 incidents, Provider shall provide status updates no less frequently than every sixty (60) minutes until service restoration.

actions.replacementText: Provider may suspend only affected tenants, accounts, or credentials where feasible, and shall provide prior notice unless immediate action is required for security or legal compliance.

actions.supplementDraft: Provider shall maintain an internal suspension log with timestamp, trigger, scope, and restoration steps and share a summary upon Customer request.
During suspension for non-payment, Provider shall permit limited read-only data export unless prohibited by law or security considerations.
Where transfer impact assessments are legally required, Provider shall provide commercially reasonable assistance and relevant transfer documentation.

actions.replacementText: Customer may request one annual security review package, including certifications, penetration-test summary findings, and remediation status, subject to confidentiality safeguards.
Provider shall notify Customer within seventy-two (72) hours of confirmed Security Incident impacting Customer Data, including scope, known impact, and immediate containment actions.

actions.supplementDraft: Provider shall provide a written root-cause analysis within ten (10) business days after containment, unless otherwise agreed for complex incidents.

actions.replacementText: Customer owns customer-specific outputs generated from Customer prompts and data. Provider retains rights in underlying models, tools, and generalized improvements that do not disclose Customer Confidential Information.

actions.supplementDraft: If Provider elects termination as an IP remedy, Provider shall provide reasonable transition assistance for up to sixty (60) days at standard professional-services rates.

actions.replacementText: Except for excluded liabilities, each party's aggregate liability shall not exceed one hundred and twenty percent (120%) of fees paid in the twelve (12) months preceding the event giving rise to the claim.

actions.supplementDraft: For Security Incident claims involving unauthorized disclosure of Customer Data, Provider's liability cap shall be one hundred and fifty percent (150%) of fees paid in the prior twelve (12) months.

actions.replacementText: Customer may export Customer Data for sixty (60) days following termination. Provider shall delete Customer Data from active systems within ninety (90) days unless retention is required by law.

actions.supplementDraft: Provider shall certify completion of deletion in writing upon Customer request, identifying retained datasets and legal basis for retention where applicable.
